blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: That's the move. Don't overthink it. Just exist near her.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: BRUH. You really out here choosing tryouts over a senior girl who's asking about you?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Homie she's a senior who asked WHO ARE YOU after one day. That's not 'not thinking about you like that.' Trust me fr.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Bruh you went quiet over a girl that likes you. You are so broken dawg.
Third period. English. Crystal sat two rows over, one seat up. I'd noticed her before — of course I had. Dark hair, quiet in a way that didn't feel like shyness, more like she was deciding something.
She didn't look over. But she'd been looking. That's what Jonathan said.

blocks.speaker: Ms. Barrett
blocks.content: Chapter four. Someone summarize the last ten pages.
Crystal raised her hand before anyone else. Of course she did.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: The narrator keeps describing what other people have. Never what he wants. It's like he can't picture himself having anything so he just observes everyone else.
The room went quiet for a second. Ms. Barrett nodded slowly like Crystal had said something she'd been waiting for someone to say.
I wrote it down. I didn't know why.
